Call (Call Us)

Stump Grinding -- Clear the Yard, Not Just the Tree

Serving Cartersville, GA and the surrounding area.

Call (Call Us) Get a Free Estimate

A tree removal isn't finished until the stump is gone. Left in the ground, a stump is a mowing hazard, a tripping hazard, and eventually a home for termites and carpenter ants that can migrate toward your house. It also keeps you from replanting, regrading, or laying sod in that spot.

We grind stumps across Bartow County as a standalone service or bundled with a removal job. Pricing typically runs $100 to $400 per stump, depending on diameter, root flare size, and how deep you want the grind -- surface-level for mowing convenience, or deeper if you're planning to replant a tree or shrub in the same spot.

Red clay soil in this area tends to hold onto root systems tighter than sandy soil, which means bigger stumps -- especially oaks and sweetgums -- take more grinding time than the same size stump would in looser ground. Pine stumps generally grind faster; their root structure is shallower and less dense.

The grinder chews the stump and the visible root flare down to below grade, leaving a mulch pile of wood chips where the stump was. We can haul that mulch off or leave it -- some homeowners want it for garden beds, others want the spot completely clear. Either way, most of the root system below the grind line stays in the ground and breaks down naturally over the following seasons; it won't sprout new growth once the stump itself is ground out.

Multiple stumps on one property -- common after a storm takes down several trees at once, or after clearing a fence line -- usually get a per-stump discount since the grinder's already on site and set up. If you've got 3 or more stumps, mention it when you call and we'll quote the batch rate instead of pricing each one separately.

One thing to flag before we grind: buried utility lines, irrigation, or septic lines near the stump. Call Georgia 811 before any major digging on your property, and let us know if you already know where lines run -- it keeps the job on schedule and avoids an expensive mistake.

If you're planning to sell the property or just want the yard to look finished after a removal, grinding is the difference between a lawn that mows clean and one with a hazard bump every homeowner trips over eventually. It's a small add-on cost relative to the removal itself, and most customers do it same-visit rather than scheduling it separately.

Common Questions

How much does stump grinding cost?

Typically $100 to $400 per stump, based on diameter and root flare size. A small pine stump is on the low end; a large oak or sweetgum with a wide root flare costs more because it takes longer to grind down. Multiple stumps on the same property usually get a per-stump discount.

Will the stump grow back?

No. Grinding removes the stump and visible root flare below grade so it can't resprout. Some smaller surface roots may remain and slowly decompose over a season or two, but they won't produce new growth once the stump itself is gone.

Do you haul away the wood chip mulch?

Either way works -- we can haul the mulch off site, or leave it in a pile if you want it for garden beds or landscaping. Just let us know your preference before we start.

Can you grind a stump close to my house or fence?

Yes, in most cases -- the grinder is a compact, controllable machine that can work in tight spaces. We'll check for buried utility lines first (call Georgia 811 ahead of the appointment if you know underground lines are nearby) and confirm clearance before grinding close to a structure or fence line.
